title_en: A century on, Pilates flexes its muscles
title_zh: 百年后，普拉提展现新生命力
rubric_en: An old exercise method is newly trendy
rubric_zh: 一项古老的健身方法重新流行起来

paragraphs.en: ATTENDING A Pilates class means reckoning with your own feebleness. The Reformer machine—a moving carriage attached to various springs and straps—looks rather like a torture device (and, depending on your instructor’s inclination, it can be). The idea is to perform slow, controlled movements such as lunges using springs as resistance; it does not sound too bad. But then, out of nowhere, you are dripping with sweat and your knees are shaking as if you are performing a bad Elvis impersonation.
paragraphs.zh: 上普拉提课程的体验，往往先是和自己的虚弱感做一次对照。普拉提器械（Reformer）是一种带有活动小车、弹簧和带子的设备，外观上很像拷问装置（而且，取决于你的教练风格，它真的可能就是）。训练理念是用弹簧提供阻力，做缓慢、受控的动作，比如弓步。听起来并不算太糟。可一旦开始，出乎意料地全身冒汗，双膝开始发抖，仿佛你在做一场拙劣的猫王模仿秀。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps it is cool to be humbled, because Pilates—which can be performed on a mat as well as a machine—is absolutely on fire (emphasis on the abs). It was the fastest-growing workout type worldwide in 2025 on ClassPass, a fitness-session booking platform. Earlier this year Google searches for Reformer Pilates hit an all-time high. On social media videos often go viral—particularly ones of muscly men struggling and cursing through a session.
paragraphs.zh: 也许被“按在地上摩擦”反而很酷，因为普拉提——既可以在垫子上练，也可以在器械上练——现在正处于火热之中（重点是腹部）。在健身课程预订平台 ClassPass 上，它是 2025 年全球增长最快的锻炼方式。就在今年早些时候，“Reformer Pilates”的谷歌搜索量也达到了历史新高。社交媒体上的相关视频常常走红，尤其是那些肌肉男在训练时吃力、面露难色、边做边咒骂的片段。

paragraphs.en: MMCG, a property consultancy, has estimated that Club Pilates, America’s largest studio franchise, grew from fewer than 50 branches in 2015 to more than 1,100 in 2024. “People are opening Pilates studios the way they opened frozen yogurt places and car washes,” says Maria Leone of Bodyline, a studio in Los Angeles, a city renowned for toned, beach-ready bodies.
paragraphs.zh: MMCG（迈克·麦克格？）这家物业咨询公司估计，Club Pilates——美国规模最大的普拉提工作室连锁——从 2015 年不到 50 家门店，增长到 2024 年的 1100 多家。洛杉矶一家名为 Bodyline 的普拉提工作室负责人玛利亚·莱昂（Maria Leone）说：“人们开设普拉提工作室的热潮，就像当年开冻酸奶店和洗车店一样。”洛杉矶这座以线条分明、上岸即战斗型身材闻名的城市，恰恰凸显了这种趋势。

paragraphs.en: This is not to say Pilates is new: in fact, Joseph Pilates (pictured below) opened the first studio in New York in 1926. A German fitness fanatic, he devised the system while interned in a British camp during the first world war. He initially dubbed his exercises, which strengthen muscles but minimise stress on the joints, “Contrology”. Unsurprisingly the name did not stick.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不代表普拉提是新的：实际上，约瑟夫·普拉提（图见下方）在 1926 年于纽约开设了第一家工作室。这位德国健身狂热者在第一次世界大战期间被关押在英国营地时，就设计出了这套方法。它最初以“Contrology（控制法）”命名，因为动作能增强肌肉却尽量减少关节压力。这个名字没有被大众接受也就不足为奇了。

paragraphs.en: The appeal of Pilates, then and now, is its emphasis on power and flexibility. The rewards of strength training are widely known and this format appeals to women who may not enjoy pumping iron in the gym, reckons Bethan Dando, a Pilates instructor. Its reputation for improving mobility, aiding muscle recovery and strengthening joints also makes it an effective complement to weightlifting, which could explain the rising interest from men. It has been endorsed by athletes including LeBron James, an American basketball player, and George Ford, an English rugby player, who reckon Pilates helps with injury rehabilitation and keeps them supple.
paragraphs.zh: 皮拉提斯过去和现在都吸引人的地方，在于它强调力量与柔韧性。皮拉提斯教练贝森·丹多（Bethan Dando）指出，力量训练的好处早已广为人知，而这种形式也受到许多不喜欢在健身房里举铁的女性欢迎。它以提高身体活动度、帮助肌肉恢复和强化关节而闻名，也使其成为举重的有效补充，这也许能解释男性兴趣的上升。它还得到了包括美国篮球运动员勒布朗·詹姆斯（LeBron James）和英国橄榄球运动员乔治·福特（George Ford）在内的多位运动员认可，他们认为皮拉提斯有助于运动损伤的康复，并能让身体保持灵活。

paragraphs.en: Can the craze stretch further? Jeff Bladt of Playlist, ClassPass’s parent company, says that in some markets supply growth is outpacing that of demand, so a saturation point may be near. Many tremble at the cost: in New York, a single Reformer class will set you back around $50. But the market is not hamstrung yet. Its fans may feel weak, but the Pilates brand is strong. ■
paragraphs.zh: 这股热潮还能继续蔓延吗？Playlist（ClassPass 的母公司）的杰夫·布拉德（Jeff Bladt）表示，在一些市场里，供应增长快于需求，因此可能即将接近饱和点。许多人对价格感到胆怯：在纽约，一节 Reformer 课通常要花约50美元。但这个市场尚未被扼杀。它的爱好者也许看上去并不强壮，但皮拉提斯品牌本身却很强。■
